events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in. All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' events.title: How to Write Non-fiction
events.description: Writing nonfiction can be a challenge but author Erin Silver will make it fun and easy! We will discuss what nonfiction writing is, how we can make it sound more like fiction, and how to organize and write engaging A+ nonfiction! Erin will share insider tips about her process, where her ideas come from, and will also read from her nonfiction book, Rush Hour: Navigating Our Global Traffic Jam .

Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing a

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Photo Editing and Management with Adobe Lightroom Classic
events.description: Adobe Lightroom Classic is an industry standard tool used by photographers to organize, retouch, their work. We will focus on learning the workflow of the software, covering topics such as importing photos, file organization, retouching and correcting our images, and exporting and sharing our finished work. Registration is required.  Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a),

events.title: Henna Art for Teens
events.description: Discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna.  Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir.  Tuesday July 21st at 6:00pm.      Ages 13-17.  Online registration is required beginning June 30th.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! For children age 9+. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Publ

events.title: Astronomer Meet & Greet
events.description: An opportunity for children, older teens and young adults to meet with an astronomer who is versed in a variety of topics. Attendees will learn about astronomy and possible careers in the field and will be able to enjoy a hands-on demonstration involving meteorites! Registration begins June 1, 2026. events.title: Sushi Making Workshop
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of sushi making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. Ages 13 – 17

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop for Teens

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
